text.title: fine-tuning-openai-dpo Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for fine-tuning-openai-dpo as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Applying DPO directly to a base model without SFT first.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: DPO requires a stable reference model. Base models without SFT produce erratic DPO results — the model has no consistent behavioral baseline to drift from. Always SFT first on the task, then apply DPO for preference alignment.

failure-mode.description: Using manually fabricated non-preferred responses that are obviously wrong.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: DPO learns from the contrast between preferred and non-preferred. If non-preferred responses are nonsense or factually wrong, the model learns a trivial distinction (coherent vs incoherent) rather than the intended preference dimension. Generate non-preferred from the base model to ensure realistic negatives.

failure-mode.description: Setting beta too low (below 0.05) without regression testing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Very low beta allows the DPO model to drift far from the SFT reference. This improves preference metrics but can catastrophically degrade SFT-quality tasks (factual accuracy, format compliance). Always eval on both preference targets and SFT-quality tasks before deploying.
